description: Italian-American Mafia crime family operating in New England, with factions in Providence, Rhode Island and Boston, Massachusetts. Founded around 1916 by Gaspare Messina, it was led by Raymond Patriarca from 1954 to 1984. The family engages in illegal gambling, loansharking, extortion, and other rackets. As of 2024, it has approximately 30 made members.

country_links.evidence_quote: He avoided prosecution and fled to Italy, remaining a fugitive for 11 years before turning himself in on August 14, 1995.
country_links.context: A Patriarca soldier fled to Italy to avoid prosecution.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Patriarca crime family ... is an Italian-American Mafia crime family operating in New England. The family consists of two distinct factions, one based in Providence, Rhode Island, and the other in Boston, Massachusetts. The Patriarca family is active in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, New Hampshire, Maine and Connecticut.
country_links.context: The family is based in and operates across multiple U.S. states including Massachusetts, Rhode Island, New Hampshire, Maine, and Connecticut.
